leadText: The Luxury Ball (Japanese: ゴージャスボール Gorgeous Ball) is a type of Poké Ball introduced in Generation III. It can be used to catch a wild Pokémon. Pokémon kept in a Luxury Ball gain friendship more quickly, including Pokémon that are bred to inherit one as their Poké Ball.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Manual activation 1.2 As a Pokémon's Poké Ball 1.3 Held item Effect [ edit | edit source ] Manual activation [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag in a wild encounter, it attempts to catch the wild Pokémon with a catch rate modifier of 1×. If used on an Ultra Beast , the catch rate modifier is instead always set to 410/4096× (~0.1×). The Luxury Ball cannot be used in situations in which Poké Balls cannot be used, such as in wild battles with two or more opponents currently present or against a trial Pokémon. If used in a Trainer battle (except if used as a Snag Ball on a Shadow Pokémon ), the opposing Trainer will deflect the ball, wasting the player's turn (as well as the item prior to Generation IV). If used on the ghost Marowak , it will dodge the ball, wasting it. As a Pokémon's Poké Ball [ edit | edit source ] Any increase to the friendship of a Pokémon in a Luxury Ball is boosted by 1. Held item [ edit | edit source ] Fling fails if the user is holding a Luxury Ball.

In Pokémon Black 2 and White 2 , during the Funfest Mission Enjoy Shopping! , Luxury Balls can be purchased for $ 400. [ edit | edit source ] Games Event Language/Region Distribution period OR AS Pokémon Scrap items Japanese December 1, 2014 to May 15, 2015 NPC usage [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon [ edit | edit source ] Gentlemen , Madames , and Trainers who share their models keep their Pokémon in Luxury Balls. Pokémon Sword and Shield [ edit | edit source ] Sordward and Shielbert keep their Pokémon in Luxury Balls. Additionally, the Toxel the player can obtain as a gift on Route 5 is contained in a Luxury Ball. The following Trainer classes keep their Pokémon in Luxury Balls: Gentleman Madame Musician Pokémon Scarlet and Violet [ edit | edit source ] Ortega and O'Nare keep their Pokémon in Luxury Balls. Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Jacinthe keeps her Pokémon in Luxury Balls.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The Luxury Ball is the only Poké Ball with a permanent, persistent effect on the Pokémon caught with it. Prior to Generation III , no Poké Ball had a persistent effect on the Pokémon it was caught in, nor was the type of ball displayed in a Pokémon's summary . The introduction of the Luxury Ball, and its associated effect, was only possible due to the index number of the Poké Ball a Pokémon was obtained in being permanently stored within a Pokémon's data structure , starting in Generation III onward.
